verses.latin_vulgate: Quemadmodum desiderat cervus ad fontes aquarum,  ita desiderat anima mea ad te, Deus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּאַיָּ֗ל תַּעֲרֹ֥ג עַל־אֲפִֽיקֵי־מָ֑יִם כֵּ֤ן נַפְשִׁ֨י תַעֲרֹ֖ג אֵלֶ֣יךָ אֱלֹהִֽים׃
verses.king_james: As the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sitivit anima mea ad Deum fortem, vivum ;  quando veniam, et apparebo ante faciem Dei ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צָמְאָ֬ה נַפְשִׁ֨י ׀ לֵאלֹהִים֮ לְאֵ֢ל חָ֥֫י מָתַ֥י אָב֑וֹא וְ֝אֵרָאֶ֗ה פְּנֵ֣י אֱלֹהִֽים׃
verses.king_james: My soul thirsteth for God, for the living God: when shall I come and appear before God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Fuerunt mihi lacrimæ meæ panes die ac nocte,  dum dicitur mihi quotidie : Ubi est Deus tuus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָֽיְתָה־לִּ֬י דִמְעָתִ֣י לֶ֭חֶם יוֹמָ֣ם וָלָ֑יְלָה בֶּאֱמֹ֥ר אֵלַ֥י כׇּל־הַ֝יּ֗וֹם אַיֵּ֥ה אֱלֹהֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: My tears have been my meat day and night, while they continually say unto me, Where is thy God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc recordatus sum, et effudi in me animam meam,  quoniam transibo in locum tabernaculi admirabilis, usque ad domum Dei,  in voce exsultationis et confessionis, sonus epulantis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֤לֶּה אֶזְכְּרָ֨ה ׀ וְאֶשְׁפְּכָ֬ה עָלַ֨י ׀ נַפְשִׁ֗י כִּ֤י אֶעֱבֹ֨ר ׀ בַּסָּךְ֮ אֶדַּדֵּ֗ם עַד־בֵּ֥ית־אֱלֹ֫הִ֥ים בְּקוֹל־רִנָּ֥ה וְתוֹדָ֗ה הָמ֥וֹן חוֹגֵֽג׃
verses.king_james: When I remember these things, I pour out my soul in me: for I had gone with the multitude, I went with them to the house of God, with the voice of joy and praise, with a multitude that kept holyday.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare tristis es, anima mea ?  et quare conturbas me ?  Spera in Deo, quoniam adhuc confitebor illi,  salutare vultus mei,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־תִּשְׁתּ֬וֹחֲחִ֨י ׀ נַפְשִׁי֮ וַתֶּהֱמִ֢י עָ֫לָ֥י הוֹחִ֣לִי לֵ֭אלֹהִים כִּי־ע֥וֹד אוֹדֶ֗נּוּ יְשׁוּע֥וֹת פָּנָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted in me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him for the help of his countenance.

verses.latin_vulgate: et Deus meus.  Ad meipsum anima mea conturbata est :  propterea memor ero tui de terra Jordanis et Hermoniim a monte modico.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱֽלֹהַ֗י עָלַי֮ נַפְשִׁ֢י תִשְׁתּ֫וֹחָ֥ח עַל־כֵּ֗ן אֶ֭זְכׇּרְךָ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ יַרְדֵּ֑ן וְ֝חֶרְמוֹנִ֗ים מֵהַ֥ר מִצְעָֽר׃
verses.king_james: O my God, my soul is cast down within me: therefore will I remember thee from the land of Jordan, and of the Hermonites, from the hill Mizar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abyssus abyssum invocat, in voce cataractarum tuarum ;  omnia excelsa tua, et fluctus tui super me transierunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּהוֹם־אֶל־תְּה֣וֹם ק֭וֹרֵא לְק֣וֹל צִנּוֹרֶ֑יךָ כׇּֽל־מִשְׁבָּרֶ֥יךָ וְ֝גַלֶּ֗יךָ עָלַ֥י עָבָֽרוּ׃
verses.king_james: Deep calleth unto deep at the noise of thy waterspouts: all thy waves and thy billows are gone over me.

verses.latin_vulgate: In die mandavit Dominus misericordiam suam,  et nocte canticum ejus ;  apud me oratio Deo vitæ meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יוֹמָ֤ם ׀ יְצַוֶּ֬ה יְהֹוָ֨ה ׀ חַסְדּ֗וֹ וּ֭בַלַּיְלָה שִׁירֹ֣ה עִמִּ֑י תְּ֝פִלָּ֗ה לְאֵ֣ל חַיָּֽי׃
verses.king_james: Yet the Lord will command his lovingkindness in the daytime, and in the night his song shall be with me, and my prayer unto the God of my life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicam Deo : Susceptor meus es ;  quare oblitus es mei ?  et quare contristatus incedo, dum affligit me inimicus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אוֹמְרָ֤ה ׀ לְאֵ֥ל סַלְעִי֮ לָמָ֢ה שְׁכַ֫חְתָּ֥נִי לָֽמָּה־קֹדֵ֥ר אֵלֵ֗ךְ בְּלַ֣חַץ אוֹיֵֽב׃
verses.king_james: I will say unto God my rock, Why hast thou forgotten me? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dum confringuntur ossa mea,  exprobraverunt mihi qui tribulant me inimici mei,  dum dicunt mihi per singulos dies : Ubi est Deus tuus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּרֶ֤צַח ׀ בְּֽעַצְמוֹתַ֗י חֵרְפ֥וּנִי צוֹרְרָ֑י בְּאׇמְרָ֥ם אֵלַ֥י כׇּל־הַ֝יּ֗וֹם אַיֵּ֥ה אֱלֹהֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: As with a sword in my bones, mine enemies reproach me; while they say daily unto me, Where is thy God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare tristis es, anima mea ?  et quare conturbas me ?  Spera in Deo, quoniam adhuc confitebor illi,  salutare vultus mei, et Deus meus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־תִּשְׁתּ֬וֹחֲחִ֨י ׀ נַפְשִׁי֮ וּֽמַה־תֶּהֱמִ֢י עָ֫לָ֥י הוֹחִ֣ילִי לֵ֭אלֹהִים כִּי־ע֣וֹד אוֹדֶ֑נּוּ יְשׁוּעֹ֥ת פָּ֝נַ֗י וֵאלֹהָֽי׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him, who is the health of my countenance, and my God.
